fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Massachusetts CNA Certification Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to Massachusetts - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity massachusetts, how to transfer CNA license to massachusetts, massachusetts CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in Massachusetts.

steps.title: Complete Training Program
steps.description: Complete a state-approved CNA training program (typically 75-120 hours).

steps.title: Pass the State Competency Exam
steps.description: Pass both the written and clinical skills portions of your state's nurse aide competency exam. There is no national certification exam for CNAs.

steps.title: Submit Application and Fees
steps.description: Complete the state application, undergo a background check if required, and submit the necessary fees.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: Who administers the Massachusetts CNA exam?
faqs.answer: The MA CNA exam is administered by D&S Headmaster. Contact them at (888) 401-0462 or visit hdmaster.com (Massachusetts CNA section).

faqs.question: How do I transfer my CNA to Massachusetts?
faqs.answer: Contact the MA DPH Nurse Aide Testing Office at (781) 979-4010 or 1-800-962-4337 for reciprocity applications. A CORI background check is required.

faqs.question: How do I verify a Massachusetts CNA?
faqs.answer: Use the NARIS (Nurse Aide Registry Information System) at nars.dph.mass.gov or call (617) 753-8144 / email nars@mass.gov.

reciprocity.notes: Contact the MA DPH Nurse Aide Testing Office at (781) 979-4010 or 1-800-962-4337 for reciprocity. A CORI (Criminal Offender Record Information) background check is required.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a CNA in MA.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to Massachusetts.

hero.title: Becoming a CNA in Massachusetts
hero.dek: Learn MA DPH requirements, complete a 100-hour program, and pass the Prometric exam.

seo.title: How to Become a CNA in Massachusetts
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to Massachusetts CNA certification.
